# Daniel Fox

> Daniel Fox is a fractional CMO and marketing leader for established, profitable companies (typically $3M-$12M revenue) selling a considered, high-ticket purchase. The work is to run the whole marketing function - strategy through pipeline - and answer for the number, rather than hand a business another vendor doing activity without leadership. He founded, built, and sold Skreened, a custom-apparel company, and has run dozens of fractional engagements since.

## Start here

- [Fractional CMO for Your Next Stage of Growth](https://daniel-fox.com/): Fractional CMO and marketing leadership for established, profitable companies — I run the whole marketing function, strategy through pipeline. Marketing for your next stage of growth.
- [What a Fractional CMO Runs — the Whole Function](https://daniel-fox.com/what-we-run.html): What a fractional CMO runs: the entire marketing function — strategy, content, SEO, AI-search visibility, paid, capture, nurture, creative, and measurement — with one number to hold.
- [Is a Fractional CMO Right for Your Business?](https://daniel-fox.com/who-its-for.html): When a fractional CMO is the right call for a considered, high-ticket business — and when it isn't. A plain, honest fit check.
- [About Daniel Fox — Fractional CMO & Operator](https://daniel-fox.com/about.html): I'm Daniel Fox — a fractional CMO who has personally run the marketing function for dozens of high-ticket businesses, and who built and sold a company of my own.
- [Book a Free Diagnostic with a Fractional CMO](https://daniel-fox.com/contact.html): Tell me about your business in two minutes. A short conversation tells us both whether the math and the fit are there. No pitch deck, no pressure.

## How demand works (pillar and guides)

- [Demand Generation for High-Ticket Buyers](https://daniel-fox.com/demand-generation-for-high-ticket-businesses.html): How to generate steady demand for a considered, high-ticket purchase — and why owning the whole marketing function beats paying for scattered activity that never compounds.
- [Fractional CMO vs. Agency: Which Do You Need?](https://daniel-fox.com/fractional-cmo-vs-marketing-agency.html): Agency, freelancer, or fractional marketing leader? A plain breakdown for owners of high-ticket businesses on who actually owns the outcome — and who just runs tasks.
- [SEO for High-Ticket Businesses](https://daniel-fox.com/seo-for-high-ticket-businesses.html): How a high-ticket business wins search for a researched, considered purchase — and why content and SEO only compound when one leader runs the plan. A fractional CMO's view.
- [Getting Found in AI Search Results](https://daniel-fox.com/getting-found-by-ai-search.html): When buyers ask an assistant to recommend a business like yours, will it name you? A clear look at earning visibility in AI answers without chasing vanity metrics.
- [How to Generate Leads Beyond Referrals](https://daniel-fox.com/lead-generation-beyond-referrals.html): Real lead generation for high-ticket businesses — beyond referrals and luck — and why a fenced channel you can measure is the one that scales. A fractional CMO's view.

## Quick answers

- [Fractional CMO Insights — Demand for High-Ticket Businesses](https://daniel-fox.com/insights.html): How demand actually works for considered, high-ticket purchases — the fractional-CMO category argument, plus plain answers to the questions high-ticket business owners ask next.
- [Do I Need a Fractional CMO or an Agency?](https://daniel-fox.com/do-i-need-a-fractional-cmo-or-an-agency.html): Agencies execute inside your current answers about the customer. A fractional CMO reopens those answers. If your answers are producing your ceiling, execution isn't the constraint. Daniel Fox.
- [Why Is My ROAS Declining When Nothing Changed?](https://daniel-fox.com/why-is-my-roas-declining.html): Return on ad spend slipping with steady spend and unchanged campaigns usually means your perfect-fit cohort is saturating. Why the dashboard can't tell saturation from noise, and what to do.
- [How Do I Know If My Market Is Saturated?](https://daniel-fox.com/how-do-i-know-if-my-market-is-saturated.html): A saturating market and a market you address wrong produce the same silence. The wrong market won't tell you they're the wrong market. How to separate the two. Daniel Fox, fractional CMO.
- [Why Aren't My Ads Converting With a New Audience?](https://daniel-fox.com/why-arent-my-ads-converting-with-a-new-audience.html): The messaging that built trust with your first audience is actively disqualifying the next one. Trust-builders become door-closers. What to do instead. Daniel Fox, fractional CMO.
- [Should I Rebrand or Reposition?](https://daniel-fox.com/should-i-rebrand-or-reposition.html): Most rebrands are an expensive way to avoid a sentence-level fix. The adjacent market usually needs a reframe, not a new identity. Run the cheap experiment first. Daniel Fox, fractional CMO.

## Essays

- [Product-Market Fit Has a Ceiling](https://daniel-fox.com/product-market-fit-has-a-ceiling.html): The fit that got you to your first few million covers one segment, one channel, one story. Why product-market fit is a gradient, not a finish line, and how the next fit hides in plain sight.
- [The Small Tweak That Opens the Next Market](https://daniel-fox.com/the-small-tweak-that-opens-the-next-market.html): When growth stalls, founders reach for a rebrand. Usually the next market needs a reframe, not a new story. How to change one variable and reach the adjacent cohort.
- [Your Answers About the Customer Are Working as Designed](https://daniel-fox.com/your-answers-are-working-as-designed.html): Your answer to 'who is our customer' is calibrated to produce exactly your current numbers. Why the question gets filed as settled, and how to reopen it deliberately.
- [Your Dashboard Can't Tell You What's Wrong](https://daniel-fox.com/your-dashboard-cant-tell-you-whats-wrong.html): Declining return on ad spend has three different causes and one identical-looking graph. Why analytics flag the symptom, and where the real diagnosis actually happens.

## Vocabulary

- [What I Believe About Why Growth Stalls](https://daniel-fox.com/beliefs.html): Seven positions on why a profitable company hits a marketing ceiling, and why the answer that built your revenue is the one keeping it where it is. Daniel Fox, fractional CMO.
- [Projective Empathy: Reading Your Marketing From the Buyer's Seat](https://daniel-fox.com/projective-empathy.html): Projective empathy is the trained practice of inhabiting a buyer's value structure and reading your own marketing from inside it. A method by Daniel Fox, fractional CMO, drawing on Spiral Dynamics.
